items.q: How is economic growth shown on a Production Possibility Curve?
items.correct: By an outward shift of the entire curve — reflecting an increase in productive capacity through more or better resources, technology, or skills.
items.misconception: Growth is shown by moving from inside the curve to a point on the curve.
items.random_wrong: Growth is shown by the curve becoming straighter.
items.why: Moving from inside to on the curve is RECOVERY (using existing resources better), not growth. True growth requires the curve to shift outward — students confuse recovery from inefficiency with new capacity.

items.q: What is the difference between nominal and real values?
items.correct: Nominal values are measured in current money; real values are adjusted for inflation to allow meaningful comparison over time.
items.misconception: Nominal means estimated and real means accurate.
items.random_wrong: Nominal values are written and real values are spent.
items.why: Both nominal and real values are 'accurate' — they answer different questions. Real values strip out inflation to reveal actual purchasing power changes. Critical for comparing wages or GDP across years.

items.correct: A cost imposed on a third party not involved in the transaction — e.g. pollution from a factory affects neighbours who didn't choose to bear it.
items.misconception: A negative externality is something bad that happens to a business.
items.random_wrong: A negative externality is a tax on a business.
items.why: The 'externality' is external to the market participants — borne by THIRD parties. Students miss the structure: producer, consumer, then someone else who didn't agree to be affected.

items.q: The Human Development Index combines life expectancy, education and:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: GNI per capita. “GDP per capita” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Sen argued development should be measured as:
items.why: Correct answer: Capabilities. “GDP” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did Sen publish 'Development as Freedom'?
items.why: The real figure is 1999 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
